page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sgn7i-vws-m2.xlarge is a virtualized server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8369B processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.88 GB per core. For graphics and parallel computing acceleration, it includes a fractional NVIDIA A10 GPU with a 0.0833 allocation and 2 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance mostly in the average tier. The Intel Xeon CPU achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1904.08 and a multi-core score of 7176.28. In Geekbench testing, it records a single-core score of 1554.0 and a multi-core score of 5066.0, with average performance in ray tracing at 6510.0 and image processing at 4513.0. However, memory performance shows some limitations, with a PassMark memory score of 1651.18 and database operations at 2055.62, both categorized as weak. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is also relatively poor at 21.1 GB/sec. Conversely, Redis SET operations perform well at 8.23 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 9.28 GB/sec.

The fractional GPU allocation makes this instance a cost-effective option for workloads that require GPU acceleration but do not need the full resource capacity or cost of an entire dedicated GPU. It is well-suited for entry-level graphics rendering, lightweight virtual workstations, and small-scale AI inference, as demonstrated by its average performance of 111.74 tokens per second for text generation on a small 135M LLM. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases or heavy multi-threaded decompression tasks due to its weaker memory subsystem benchmarks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sgn7i-vws-m2.xlarge is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs operating at 2.9 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a fractional NVIDIA A10 GPU providing 2 GB of VRAM. Lacking local storage, it relies on network-attached storage and features a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates average CPU performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 5066.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 7176.28. However, memory bandwidth and database operations are relatively weak. The fractional GPU makes this instance a cost-efficient choice for entry-level GPU-accelerated tasks, such as lightweight virtual workstations, basic image processing, and small-scale language model inference, while avoiding the higher cost of a full dedicated GPU.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sgn7i-vws-m2.xlarge is a KVM virtual server with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a fractional NVIDIA A10 GPU with 2 GB VRAM, designed for entry-level accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sgn7i-vws-m2.xlarge features 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a fractional NVIDIA A10 GPU for budget-friendly accelerated computing.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining dedicated Intel Xeon processors with fractional NVIDIA A10 GPU acceleration for lightweight visual and compute workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8369B processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs at 2.9 GHz
Fractional NVIDIA A10 GPU with 2 GB VRAM for entry-level acceleration
16.0 GB of system memory and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average performance in CPU benchmarks and small-scale LLM inference
Weaker memory bandwidth and database operation performance
Best suited for lightweight virtual workstations and basic image processing
